events.reason: Full candidate profiles + canary matrix are receipt-proven (13 sandboxed runs, real counts, private identity); the closure-commit profile and disposable README-only clone lifecycle require the committed/pushed tree and close during the post-archive publish verification

exploitability.capability: modify tracked validation policy and outgoing Git history
exploitability.boundary: ordinary local validate and pre-push boundary
exploitability.harm: self-approve weakened checks, expose ambient credentials, or transfer hidden secrets
exploitability.fix: add trusted-policy CAS promotion, outgoing-object scanning, sandboxed children, deterministic notes/tool trust, and Build-origin artifact identity

exploitability.attacker: malicious or defective first-root candidate coordinator
exploitability.capability: execute pretrust control-plane code, omit reviewed evidence, or exploit partial activation and token replay
exploitability.boundary: first-adoption checkpoint and all-zero trusted-policy CAS
exploitability.harm: establish incorrect trust, lose durable review provenance, or leave hooks in an unsafe split state
exploitability.fix: use an honest bounded pretrust execution boundary, typed checkpoint scope, recoverable staged activation, and domain-separated proof nonce semantics

exploitability.capability: trigger output failure around a mutating command
exploitability.boundary: atomic durable mutation and terminal rendering boundary
exploitability.harm: produce committed but apparently failed state or reported PASS without durable truth
exploitability.fix: commit identified durable result before PASS; return non-success on post-commit output loss; status reports committed truth; test both sides
